qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public GraphQL __type introspection bypasses the default introspection disablement
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is confined to the GraphQL public introspection guard in `src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js`, while the advisory describes missing class-level-permission enforcement in the LiveQuery subscription and event-delivery path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's LiveQuery class-level-permission bypass root cause.
evidence: The advisory describes unauthorized LiveQuery subscriptions and real-time event leakage caused by CLP not being enforced before subscription creation and during event delivery.
The only final `qualifying_vuln` finding is `graphql_public_introspection_bypass`, which targets `POST /graphql` and the raw `__schema` substring check in `src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js`.

The repository's LiveQuery component is a separate code path in `src/LiveQuery/ParseLiveQueryServer.ts`; the qualifying finding does not describe the LiveQuery subscription authorization or event-delivery mechanism from the advisory.
